extraction.one_line_summary: HAGA Chad won the ITT stage in Verona, beating favorite CAMPENAERTS Victor and European champion expectations, while CARAPAZ Richard secured GC without drama.
tactical_patterns: HAGA beat European TT champion CAMPENAERTS by 4 seconds despite three-week Grand Tour fatigue, defying conventional freshness advantage.
ROGLIC Primoz gained exactly enough time on LANDA Mikel to steal final podium spot by mere seconds on final stage.
CARAPAZ rode tactically perfect three weeks despite zero previous Grand Tour leadership experience, never cracking under pressure.

rider_signals.HAGA Chad: positive: Sunweb rider peaked perfectly for final ITT after anonymous three weeks, revealing strong stage-hunting TT capability late in GTs
rider_signals.CAMPENAERTS Victor: negative: European TT champion expected to dominate but lost by 4 seconds, suggesting specialization doesn't overcome three-week racing form
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: negative: Lost podium by 23 seconds on final day after being supremely confident at start, catastrophic miscalculation of required effort
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: Timed podium-stealing TT performance with surgical precision, gaining exactly 23+ seconds needed on LANDA
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: First-time GT leader never made tactical error across three weeks against vastly more experienced rivals like NIBALI
